project.name: me-website
project.description: KAIST 기계공학과 학생회(MESC) 공식 웹사이트 — 공지사항, 학습자료, 예산 투명화, 건물 층별 지도, 교수님 오피스 정보 제공

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route for snack wishlists: public GET to list all wishes, rate-limited public POST to submit a wish (with XSS stripping), and admin-protected DELETE to remove a wish.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-protected file upload API that validates MIME type and file size, extracts image dimensions via sharp, and stores images to blob storage using Vercel Blob.

nodes.name: auth.ts
nodes.summary: NextAuth.js v5 configuration exporting handlers, signIn, signOut, and auth helpers; implements credentials-based admin authentication against environment-variable-stored credentials with JWT session strategy.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses NextAuth v5 destructured export pattern; JWT session strategy avoids database session storage.

nodes.name: language-context.tsx
nodes.summary: React context and hook for Korean/English language switching; persists language preference to localStorage and provides a `t()` translation helper.

nodes.name: LanguageProvider
nodes.summary: Context provider that manages language state, syncs with localStorage, and exposes a translation function to child components.

nodes.name: useLanguage
nodes.summary: Custom hook that returns the LanguageContext value including current language, setLang, and the t() translation function.

nodes.name: links.ts
nodes.summary: Centralized data module exporting IMPORTANT_LINKS (department homepage, KAIST portal, council bylaws) and COMMUNITY_LINKS (KakaoTalk, Naver Cafe, Instagram accounts) with bilingual Korean/English labels for use across the site.

nodes.name: pathfinding.ts
nodes.summary: Floor map pathfinding library implementing Dijkstra's algorithm; provides graph parsing, adjacency building, shortest path finding, and SVG polyline point generation for building navigation.

nodes.name: findPath
nodes.summary: Dijkstra's shortest-path algorithm implementation operating on a building floor graph; returns the ordered array of node IDs from source to destination.

nodes.name: buildAdjacency
nodes.summary: Builds a bidirectional adjacency map from a graph's edge list, precomputing Euclidean distances as edge weights.

nodes.name: parseGraph
nodes.summary: Parses a JSON string into a typed floor graph object with nodes and edges arrays.

nodes.name: pathToPolylinePoints
nodes.summary: Converts a path (array of node IDs) to an SVG polyline points string by mapping node IDs to x,y coordinates.

nodes.name: polygonCentroid
nodes.summary: Computes the arithmetic centroid of a polygon from its vertex point string, used to position room labels.

nodes.name: prisma.ts
nodes.summary: Singleton Prisma client factory using the libSQL adapter; exports a shared `prisma` instance with global caching to prevent multiple connections in development hot-reload cycles.
nodes.languageNotes: Prisma v7 requires the @prisma/adapter-libsql adapter; the global caching pattern avoids connection exhaustion under Next.js hot-reload.

nodes.name: svg-parser.ts
nodes.summary: SVG utility library for floor maps: sanitizes SVG against XSS, extracts viewBox dimensions, detects rooms and facilities from text elements, and converts detected rooms to clickable region polygons.

nodes.name: sanitizeSvg
nodes.summary: Strips XSS vectors from raw SVG content by removing script tags, foreignObject elements, event handler attributes, and javascript: URLs.

nodes.name: detectRoomsAndFacilities
nodes.summary: Parses a sanitized SVG DOM to detect room numbers and facility icons from text elements, returning structured room and facility arrays with position and label data.

nodes.name: detectedRoomsToRegions
nodes.summary: Converts detected room positions to bounding-box polygon regions with optional padding, ready for interactive overlay rendering.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Provides the cn() class name utility merging clsx conditional classes with tailwind-merge deduplication, used throughout the component library.

nodes.name: validation.ts
nodes.summary: Server-side input validation utilities used by API routes to sanitize and validate URLs, strings, monetary amounts, IDs, dates, and category values before database writes.

nodes.name: isValidUrl
nodes.summary: Validates a URL string against an allowlist of permitted protocols (http, https, etc.) to prevent XSS via malicious URLs.

nodes.name: parseId
nodes.summary: Parses and validates an integer ID from a string, returning null if the value is not a valid integer.

nodes.name: prisma.config.ts
nodes.summary: Prisma v7 datasource configuration using defineConfig to set the schema path, migrations directory, and DATABASE_URL from environment variables — required because Prisma v7 does not support the url field directly in schema.prisma.
nodes.languageNotes: Prisma v7 requires datasource configuration to be declared in prisma.config.ts rather than in the schema.prisma datasource block.

nodes.name: schema.prisma
nodes.summary: Prisma ORM schema defining 13 models: Notice, Resource, BudgetItem, Member, Course, Event, EventPhoto, EventFeedback, SnackWish, PopupSettings, PopupLink, Building, BuildingFloor, and Professor; covers all core entities for the MESC student council website.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Prisma v7 with SQLite provider; datasource URL is managed in prisma.config.ts instead of here.

nodes.name: Notice
nodes.summary: Student council notice model with title, content, category, pinned flag, and timestamps.

nodes.name: Resource
nodes.summary: Learning resource model with title, file URL, category, and optional course code.

nodes.name: BudgetItem
nodes.summary: Budget transparency model tracking income/expense entries with date, description, amount, category, and optional receipt URL.

nodes.name: Member
nodes.summary: Student council member model with name, role, bureau, council flag, image URL, and ordering.

nodes.name: Building
nodes.summary: Campus building model with unique code, Korean/English name, and relations to BuildingFloor and Professor.

nodes.name: BuildingFloor
nodes.summary: Floor plan model storing image URL, dimensions, raw SVG content, room polygon regions JSON, and pathfinding graph JSON for each floor of a building.

nodes.name: Professor
nodes.summary: Professor office information model with name, title, building/floor relations, room number, contact fields, and research area.

nodes.name: SnackWish
nodes.summary: Snack wishlist entry model with text content and creation timestamp.

nodes.name: Event
nodes.summary: Student council event model with title, date, cover image, and relations to EventPhoto and EventFeedback.

nodes.name: Notice
nodes.summary: Initial CREATE TABLE statement for the Notice model establishing the base schema.

nodes.name: migration.sql
nodes.summary: Initial database migration creating all core tables: Notice, Resource, BudgetItem, Member, Course, Event, EventPhoto, EventFeedback, SnackWish, PopupSettings, and PopupLink.

nodes.name: migration.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding the Building and BuildingFloor tables with floor map geometry fields (imageUrl, dimensions, regionsJson, graphJson) and the Professor model.

nodes.name: migration.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding the svgContent column to BuildingFloor for storing raw inline SVG converted from DWG/PDF.

nodes.name: migration_lock.toml
nodes.summary: Prisma migration lock file recording the database provider (sqlite) to prevent accidental provider switches.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin server component for the floor map editor page; validates session and floor ID before rendering the FloorMapViewer editor UI for a specific building floor.

nodes.name: FloorMapEditorPage
nodes.summary: Next.js server component that guards admin access, resolves the floor record from the database, and renders the floor map editor.

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Protected admin layout component that checks NextAuth session on every request and redirects unauthenticated users to the login page.

nodes.name: ProtectedAdminLayout
nodes.summary: Server-side layout wrapper that enforces authentication; redirects to login if no active session exists.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: NextAuth.js catch-all API route with rate limiting on POST requests; exports GET and POST handlers for the credentials-based authentication flow.

nodes.name: checkLoginLimit
nodes.summary: In-memory IP-based rate limiter for login attempts; blocks IPs that exceed 5 attempts within a 60-second window.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-protected API route for adding a new floor to a specific building; validates auth and input before creating a BuildingFloor record.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-protected API route for updating (PUT) or deleting (DELETE) a specific building record by ID.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-protected API route for updating (PUT) or deleting (DELETE) a specific building floor record by floor ID.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route for listing all buildings (public GET) and creating new buildings (admin-protected POST) with their floor associations.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route handler for budget items: GET returns all budget items from the database, POST creates a new item with full input validation requiring admin authentication.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Public GET endpoint that checks whether a student ID is present in the Google Sheets fee payment list; includes IP rate limiting, 5-minute CSV cache, and automatic student-ID column detection.

nodes.name: fetchSheetData
nodes.summary: Fetches and parses the Google Sheets CSV export with a 5-minute in-memory cache; returns a 2D array of cell strings.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route handler for student council member records: GET lists all members, POST creates a new member with validation of name, role, bureau, and optional photo URL requiring admin authentication.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route for a specific notice: public GET by ID, admin-protected PUT (update) and DELETE with full input validation.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route for listing all notices (public GET) and creating new notices (admin-protected POST) with title, content, and category validation.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-protected API route for updating (PUT) or deleting (DELETE) a specific professor record by ID.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route for listing all professors with building/floor relations (public GET) and creating new professor entries (admin-protected POST).

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-protected API route for updating (PUT) or deleting (DELETE) a specific learning resource record by ID, with URL and string validation.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route for listing all learning resources (public GET) and creating new resources (admin-protected POST) with title, URL, and category validation.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-protected API route for deleting a specific snack wish entry by ID.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin budget management page allowing administrators to add, view, and delete budget items with KRW formatting and date-based categorization.

nodes.name: AdminBudgetPage
nodes.summary: Client component rendering the budget administration UI with form for creating entries and a table for managing existing budget items.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Main admin dashboard page showing navigation links to all admin sub-sections and quick links to public pages, protected by NextAuth session.

nodes.name: AdminPage
nodes.summary: Server component rendering the admin dashboard with sign-out functionality and navigation to all protected admin sections.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin page for managing student snack wish submissions, supporting individual and bulk deletion of wish list entries.

nodes.name: AdminSnackWishesPage
nodes.summary: Client component listing all snack wish submissions with options to delete individual entries or clear all at once.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin client component for managing student council member records with full CRUD operations: create, edit, delete, and reorder members with photo upload via the upload API.

nodes.name: AdminMembersPage
nodes.summary: Admin page component managing the full lifecycle of member records including form state, file upload, inline editing, submission, and deletion with optimistic UI feedback.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin notice management page providing create, edit, and delete operations for announcements with category and pinning support.

nodes.name: AdminNoticesPage
nodes.summary: Client component managing the notices admin interface with inline editing, category filtering, and pinned-post toggling.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin resources management page enabling CRUD operations for study materials linked to courses with category classification.

nodes.name: AdminResourcesPage
nodes.summary: Client component for creating and managing study resources, dynamically loading available courses to associate resources with course codes.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin professor management page with full CRUD, image upload, building/floor assignment, and live search filtering across professor records.

nodes.name: AdminProfessorsPage
nodes.summary: Client component managing professor records with building/floor relationship, searchable list, image upload, and inline form editing.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Budget transparency page displaying income/expense summary, category breakdown with percentage bars, and a paginated transaction detail table fetched from the budget-summary API with KRW formatting.

nodes.name: BudgetPage
nodes.summary: Budget transparency client component rendering financial summaries, category distribution bars, and a paginated transaction list with Korean currency formatting and i18n support.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-featured calendar page displaying monthly events fetched from the iCal API with Google Calendar subscription, iCal download, month navigation modal, and event detail list.

nodes.name: CalendarPage
nodes.summary: Main calendar component with month/year navigation, event filtering by month, Google Calendar subscription button, iCal download, and a year/month selection modal.

nodes.name: formatICalDate
nodes.summary: Parses iCal DTSTART/DTEND date strings in compact format (e.g. 20260101T120000Z) into human-readable Korean date-time strings.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Student fee payment lookup page that allows students to enter their ID and query the Google Sheets-backed check-fee API to verify membership fee payment status.

nodes.name: CheckFeePage
nodes.summary: Interactive fee verification form with loading, error, and result states that calls /api/check-fee with the student ID and displays payment status with i18n text.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Server component that fetches building and professor data from Prisma and passes it to the client-side DepartmentInfoClient component.

nodes.name: DepartmentInfoPage
nodes.summary: Async server component fetching buildings with nested floors/rooms and professors for rendering by the client component.

nodes.name: department-info-client.tsx
nodes.summary: Interactive client component for exploring department information, featuring tabbed building/floor navigation, professor search, and floor map viewing with room details.

nodes.name: DepartmentInfoClient
nodes.summary: Client component rendering building/floor tabs, SVG floor map with room overlays, and a searchable professor directory with jump-to-floor links.

nodes.name: members-client.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side React component for the members page, displaying student council members grouped by bureau and department clubs (MR, ZILZU) in a tabbed layout with i18n support, plus important links and SNS community links sections.

nodes.name: MembersClient
nodes.summary: Main exported client component that groups council members by bureau, renders them in tabs alongside department club info cards, and displays important and community link grids with bilingual labels.

nodes.name: MemberCard
nodes.summary: Renders a single member's avatar card with a circular profile image (or placeholder icon), name, and role label.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Next.js server page for the /members route that fetches all members from the database ordered by position and passes them to the MembersClient component for rendering.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Client component rendering the detail view for a single notice, fetched by ID via API with loading state and back navigation.

nodes.name: NoticeDetailPage
nodes.summary: Client component loading and displaying a notice's full content including category badge, pinned status, and formatted creation date.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Public notices listing page fetching all announcements and rendering them with category filter tabs and navigation to detail views.

nodes.name: NoticesPage
nodes.summary: Client component displaying a filterable list of notices grouped by category with pinned posts surfaced first.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Public study resources page fetching materials from the API and presenting them with category filtering, with special handling for Naver Cafe links.

nodes.name: ResourcesPage
nodes.summary: Client component rendering filterable study materials list, distinguishing Naver Cafe links to show an appropriate access notice.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Application home page server component that fetches the five most recent pinned-priority notices and renders them in the landing view.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Public-facing course listing page that fetches courses from the API, supports level filtering tabs (200/300/400/기타), and renders course cards with i18n support.

nodes.name: CoursesPage
nodes.summary: Client component displaying filterable mechanical engineering courses organized by academic level with language-aware labels.

nodes.name: globals.css
nodes.summary: Global CSS defining the design system's custom Tailwind theme tokens (oklch color palette, radius scale), utility classes (glass-premium, hover-lift-premium, tech-mesh, badge variants), and base resets with dark mode support.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses oklch color space for the color palette and Tailwind CSS v4 @theme inline syntax. Custom animations include dashMove for SVG floor map pathfinding overlays.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin page for managing campus buildings, including CRUD operations for building records and their associated floor data (levels, descriptions, image uploads, and floor map editor integration).

nodes.name: AdminBuildingsPage
nodes.summary: Client component that renders the building management interface with forms for creating/editing buildings, floor management, and launching the FloorMapEditor.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin client component for managing mechanical engineering course entries with full CRUD: create and edit courses with fields for code, level, name, description, textbook availability, YouTube link, and display order.

nodes.name: AdminCoursesPage
nodes.summary: Admin page component for course management supporting creation, inline editing, deletion, and level categorization with scrollable form reference for smooth UX.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin page for managing student council events including CRUD operations, cover image and photo gallery uploads, ZIP download of photos, and feedback review and deletion.

nodes.name: AdminEventsPage
nodes.summary: Client component orchestrating event creation, photo management, ZIP download via jszip, and feedback moderation with inline dialogs and table views.

nodes.name: FloorMapEditor.tsx
nodes.summary: Interactive SVG floor map editor component for drawing room polygons, placing navigation graph nodes/edges, and auto-detecting rooms from uploaded SVG floor plans; persists data via REST API.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses SVG coordinate transformation (createSVGPoint/getScreenCTM) for precise mouse-to-SVG-space mapping during polygon drawing.

nodes.name: FloorMapEditor
nodes.summary: Main editor component managing room polygon drawing mode, graph node/edge placement, SVG/image upload, auto-detection of rooms, and save operations for floor map data.

nodes.name: FloorMapViewer.tsx
nodes.summary: Interactive floor map viewer component that renders SVG floor plans with room polygon highlighting, professor-room associations, and A* pathfinding between navigable rooms.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses react-zoom-pan-pinch for pan/zoom and implements point-in-polygon hit testing for room selection.

nodes.name: FloorMapViewer
nodes.summary: Exported viewer component rendering an interactive SVG map with room highlighting, professor info panels, pathfinding UI, and pan/zoom controls.

nodes.name: pointInPolygon
nodes.summary: Ray-casting algorithm to determine whether a 2D point lies inside a polygon, used for click-based room selection on the floor map.

nodes.name: button.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn/UI Button component with variant and size support using class-variance-authority, providing default, destructive, outline, ghost, and link styles.

nodes.name: Button
nodes.summary: Polymorphic button component applying cva-generated variant/size classes and forwarding all native HTML button props.

nodes.name: card.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn/UI Card component family (Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardAction, CardContent, CardFooter) providing composable container layouts.

nodes.name: dialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn/UI Dialog component family wrapping Radix UI Dialog primitives with custom styling for overlay, content, header, footer, title, and description.

nodes.name: input.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn/UI Input component providing a styled HTML input element with consistent border, focus ring, and disabled-state styling.

nodes.name: badge.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn/UI Badge component with variant support (default, secondary, destructive, outline) using class-variance-authority for visual status indicators.

nodes.name: select.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn/UI Select component family wrapping Radix UI Select primitives with full keyboard accessibility, custom styling, and scroll buttons for long option lists.

nodes.name: table.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn/UI Table component family (Table, TableHeader, TableBody, TableFooter, TableRow, TableHead, TableCell, TableCaption) for rendering styled data tables.

nodes.name: textarea.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn/UI Textarea component providing a styled multi-line text input with consistent focus and disabled state styling.

nodes.name: label.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn/UI Label component wrapping a styled HTML label element for accessible form field labeling.

nodes.name: scroll-area.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn/UI ScrollArea component providing a custom-styled scrollable container, likely wrapping Radix UI ScrollArea primitives. File was not present on disk during analysis.

nodes.name: separator.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn/UI Separator component rendering a horizontal or vertical divider line with accessible role and orientation props.

nodes.name: sheet.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn/UI Sheet component providing a slide-in panel (drawer) from any screen edge, wrapping Radix UI Dialog primitives with side-specific animations. File was not present on disk during analysis.

nodes.name: slider.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn/UI Slider component providing a styled range input built on Radix UI Slider primitives. File was not present on disk during analysis.

nodes.name: tabs.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable Tabs UI component built on @base-ui-components/react with support for horizontal and vertical orientations and two visual variants (default pill background and borderless line), exporting Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, and TabsContent.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses class-variance-authority (cva) for variant-based styling and wraps @base-ui-react/tabs primitives, applying data-attribute selectors for orientation-aware layout.

nodes.name: tooltip.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn/UI Tooltip component wrapping Radix UI Tooltip primitives for accessible hover/focus tooltip overlays. File was not present on disk during analysis.

nodes.name: CardHeader
nodes.summary: Card header sub-component providing a flex container with vertical spacing for card metadata.

nodes.name: CardTitle
nodes.summary: Card title sub-component rendering a styled heading within a card.

nodes.name: CardContent
nodes.summary: Card content sub-component providing the primary body area of a card with consistent padding.

nodes.name: DialogOverlay
nodes.summary: Dialog overlay component rendering a semi-transparent backdrop with fade and zoom animations.

nodes.name: DialogHeader
nodes.summary: Dialog header sub-component providing a flex container for the dialog title and description.

nodes.name: DialogFooter
nodes.summary: Dialog footer sub-component with optional close button for action buttons at the bottom of a dialog.

nodes.name: DialogTitle
nodes.summary: Accessible dialog title component rendering a visually prominent heading with Radix Dialog.Title semantics.

nodes.name: SelectTrigger
nodes.summary: Select trigger button component displaying the selected value and a chevron icon, with size variants.

nodes.name: SelectContent
nodes.summary: Select dropdown content container with positioning alignment, offset, and scroll button support.

nodes.name: SelectItem
nodes.summary: Individual select option item with focus/selected state styling and a check indicator.

nodes.name: Table
nodes.summary: Root table component wrapping a scrollable container with consistent width and styling.

nodes.name: TableRow
nodes.summary: Table row component with hover and selected state styling for interactive data tables.

nodes.name: TableCell
nodes.summary: Table data cell component with consistent padding and vertical alignment.

nodes.name: Tabs
nodes.summary: Root tabs container component that sets orientation via a data attribute and applies flex layout direction based on horizontal or vertical mode.

nodes.name: TabsList
nodes.summary: Tabs list wrapper that applies variant-based styling (default muted background pill or transparent line) via cva variants.

nodes.name: TabsTrigger
nodes.summary: Individual tab trigger button with active state styling, focus-visible ring, disabled states, and an optional underline indicator for the line variant.

nodes.name: Separator
nodes.summary: Accessible separator/divider component with horizontal and vertical orientation support via Radix UI.

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Root Next.js layout that sets global metadata (title, description, Open Graph) and wraps all pages with the LayoutWrapper providing theme, language context, Navbar, and Footer.

nodes.name: RootLayout
nodes.summary: Default exported root layout component that renders the HTML shell and delegates child wrapping to LayoutWrapper.

nodes.name: Footer.tsx
nodes.summary: Fully internationalized site footer displaying MESC branding, SNS links, quick navigation links, resource links, and contact information sourced from lib/links.ts with Korean/English toggle via useLanguage.

nodes.name: Footer
nodes.summary: Renders the site-wide footer with four sections: SNS links, quick links, resource links, and contact info, all translated via the useLanguage hook.

nodes.name: Navbar.tsx
nodes.summary: Responsive top navigation bar with active-route highlighting, a Korean/English language toggle, and a collapsible mobile menu, using usePathname for route awareness.

nodes.name: Navbar
nodes.summary: Client-side navigation component that renders desktop and mobile nav menus with localized link labels and a language switcher.

nodes.name: layout-wrapper.tsx
nodes.summary: Client boundary wrapper that composes ThemeProvider, LanguageProvider, Navbar, and Footer around page content, serving as the single integration point for all global layout concerns.

nodes.name: LayoutWrapper
nodes.summary: Wraps children with ThemeProvider and LanguageProvider context, and renders Navbar above and Footer below the page content.

nodes.name: theme-provider.tsx
nodes.summary: Custom React context-based theme provider that persists light/dark/system preference to localStorage, listens for OS-level prefers-color-scheme changes, and exposes a useTheme hook.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the OS media-query change listener pattern to reactively sync theme when the user switches system appearance without a page reload.

nodes.name: ThemeProvider
nodes.summary: Context provider that initialises theme from localStorage, applies CSS classes to the document root, and subscribes to prefers-color-scheme media query changes.

nodes.name: useTheme
nodes.summary: Custom hook that reads from the ThemeContext and throws if used outside a ThemeProvider.

nodes.name: next.config.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js configuration defining Content Security Policy headers, allowed image domains, and other runtime settings for the MESC website.

nodes.name: TabsContent
nodes.summary: Tab panel content container that fills remaining space and removes default outline.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Agent-specific instruction file warning that the bundled Next.js version has breaking changes and directing agents to read the guide in node_modules/next/dist/docs/ before writing any code.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Collaboration instructions for Claude covering design system compliance, code style (Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, Next.js App Router), and agent usage rules including mandatory parallel sub-agent usage for complex tasks.

nodes.name: CLAUDE_COLLABORATION_GUIDE.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive full-stack UI/UX collaboration guide for Claude covering design system usage, dark mode implementation, i18n (Korean/English), and layout rules specific to the MESC website.

nodes.name: DESIGN_SYSTEM.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive design system and UI/UX guidelines for the MESC website, defining color palette (CSS custom properties), typography, component guidelines (Navbar, Footer, Card, Button, Badge), custom Tailwind utility classes, animations, and accessibility standards.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Standard Next.js project README covering getting started commands, links to Next.js documentation, and deployment instructions for Vercel.

nodes.name: MEMORY.md
nodes.summary: Claude agent memory file recording project overview, tech stack (Next.js, Prisma, NextAuth), key configuration files, page structure, important caveats about Prisma v7 + SQLite setup, and confirmed SNS links for the MESC website.

nodes.name: project_status.md
nodes.summary: Development status tracking document listing completed work (KAIST link updates, i18n, security hardening, admin UI improvements) and remaining tasks (Google Calendar/Sheets integration, privacy/terms pages, build verification, deployment).

nodes.name: next-env.d.ts
nodes.summary: Auto-generated Next.js TypeScript environment declaration file that includes Next.js type references for Image and Link components; should not be manually edited.
nodes.languageNotes: Auto-generated by Next.js; references next/types/global.d.ts and next/image-types/global.d.ts to extend the global TypeScript environment.

nodes.name: launch.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code launch configuration defining version and execution configurations for the project development environment.

nodes.name: settings.local.json
nodes.summary: Local Claude Code settings defining permission allowlists for Bash, file read/write, and MCP tool operations specific to this project.

nodes.name: components.json
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui configuration specifying the Nova style preset, RSC support, Tailwind CSS integration, path aliases for components and utilities, and the lucide icon library.
nodes.languageNotes: The Nova preset and lucide icon library are explicitly configured, enabling the project's design system component generation.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Project manifest for the MESC website declaring Next.js 16, Prisma v7, NextAuth v5, Recharts, shadcn/ui, and Vercel Blob as primary dependencies with Tailwind CSS 4 and TypeScript 5 for development.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Prisma v7 with both @prisma/adapter-libsql and @prisma/adapter-better-sqlite3 adapters for flexible SQLite deployment. The postinstall script runs prisma generate automatically.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration enabling strict mode, JSX preservation for Next.js, module resolution with path alias @/* mapping to the project root.

nodes.name: postcss.config.mjs
nodes.summary: PostCSS configuration enabling the @tailwindcss/postcss plugin for Tailwind CSS v4 processing.

nodes.name: eslint.config.mjs
nodes.summary: ESLint flat configuration extending Next.js core web vitals and TypeScript rules for code quality enforcement.

nodes.name: .gitignore
nodes.summary: Git ignore rules excluding Node modules, Next.js build artifacts, environment files, and database files from version control.

nodes.name: cn
nodes.summary: Merges Tailwind CSS class names using clsx for conditional logic and tailwind-merge to eliminate conflicting utility duplicates.

nodes.name: isValidString
nodes.summary: Validates a string is non-empty after trimming and within a configurable maximum length.

nodes.name: isValidAmount
nodes.summary: Validates a monetary amount is a finite number within an allowable range.

nodes.name: isValidDate
nodes.summary: Validates a date string by attempting to parse it as a Date object and confirming it is not NaN.

nodes.name: isAllowedCategory
nodes.summary: Validates a value against an explicit allowlist of permitted category strings.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Creates a new budget item after verifying admin session and validating all fields (date, description, amount, category, receipt URL) before persisting to the database.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Creates a new member record after verifying admin session and validating name, role, bureau, and photo URL before persisting to the database.

nodes.name: robots.txt
nodes.summary: Web crawler directive file that disallows indexing of the admin panel and all API routes, and declares the sitemap URL at the Vercel deployment domain.
nodes.languageNotes: Standard robots.txt protocol: blocks /admin and /api/ from all user-agents and references the production sitemap on mesc-website.vercel.app.

nodes.name: Card
nodes.summary: Primary Card container component providing a rounded, shadowed flex container with configurable size variants (default/sm) for composable card layouts.

nodes.name: DialogContent
nodes.summary: Dialog content popup component wrapping Radix Dialog.Popup with overlay, centered positioning, fade/zoom animations, and an optional close button.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: Next.js App Router REST API route handlers for all resources — notices, buildings, professors, budget, upload, auth, and snack wishes — serving both public and admin clients.

layers.name: Admin Panel
layers.description: Password-protected admin dashboard with CRUD management pages for notices, resources, budget, professors, buildings, floor maps, members, courses, events, and snack wishes.

layers.name: Public Pages
layers.description: Student-facing Next.js pages for the MESC website — home, notices, calendar, budget, check-fee, department info, members, resources, courses, events, and snack wishes — plus the root layout and error pages.

layers.name: UI Components
layers.description: Reusable React components providing site-wide layout (Navbar, Footer), theme and language context providers, and the interactive FloorMapViewer for building navigation.

layers.name: Service & Utility Layer
layers.description: Core library modules providing the Prisma database client, NextAuth authentication, Dijkstra pathfinding for floor maps, SVG parsing/sanitization, i18n context, input validation, and shared utility helpers.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Prisma schema and database model definitions for all 13 entities — notices, resources, budget items, snack wishes, buildings, floor maps, and professors — backed by Turso/libSQL.

layers.name: Configuration & Documentation
layers.description: Project configuration files (Next.js, Prisma, TypeScript, shadcn/ui, package.json), Claude Code workspace settings, and documentation including the design system guide, agent changelog, and robots.txt.

tour.title: 애플리케이션 진입점
tour.description: 루트 레이아웃인 app/layout.tsx는 모든 페이지가 공유하는 최상위 래퍼입니다. 폰트 로딩, ThemeProvider, LanguageProvider를 초기화하며 Navbar와 Footer를 여기서 마운트합니다. Next.js App Router에서 layout.tsx는 한 번 렌더링되어 하위 페이지 전체를 감싸기 때문에, 이 파일을 이해하면 사이트 전체 구조가 한눈에 보입니다.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js App Router의 layout.tsx는 React의 children prop을 통해 하위 page.tsx를 감쌉니다. Server Component로 동작하므로 DB 쿼리나 인증 로직을 서버에서 처리할 수 있으며, 'use client' 지시어가 없으면 기본적으로 서버 컴포넌트입니다.

tour.title: 글로벌 스타일 시스템
tour.description: app/globals.css는 Tailwind CSS 위에 프로젝트 고유의 디자인 토큰을 정의합니다. oklch 색상 공간 기반의 --primary, --accent 변수와 glass-premium, tech-mesh, hover-lift-premium 같은 커스텀 유틸리티 클래스, 그리고 dashMove 애니메이션이 여기에 선언됩니다. layout.tsx가 이 파일을 임포트하므로 모든 페이지에 자동 적용됩니다.
tour.languageLesson: CSS oklch(lightness chroma hue) 색상 모델은 sRGB보다 지각적으로 균일한 색상 표현을 제공합니다. Tailwind의 @layer utilities 블록 안에 정의된 클래스는 빌드 시 purge되지 않고 보존됩니다.

tour.title: 디자인 시스템 문서
tour.description: DESIGN_SYSTEM.md는 globals.css에 정의된 색상 토큰과 유틸리티 클래스의 사용 지침을 담은 설계 문서입니다. 컬러 팔레트, 타이포그래피 규칙, 컴포넌트별 스타일 가이드라인, 애니메이션 원칙이 기술되어 있습니다. 새 UI 요소를 추가할 때 반드시 이 문서를 먼저 참조해야 코드 전체의 시각적 일관성을 유지할 수 있습니다.

tour.title: 다국어(i18n) 컨텍스트
tour.description: lib/language-context.tsx는 한국어/영어 전환을 관리하는 React Context입니다. 선택한 언어는 localStorage에 저장되어 새로고침 후에도 유지됩니다. fan-in이 4로 가장 높은 유틸리티 중 하나로, Navbar, Footer, 부서 정보 클라이언트 페이지 등 여러 컴포넌트가 이 컨텍스트를 구독합니다.
tour.languageLesson: React Context의 createContext + useContext 패턴은 prop drilling 없이 전역 상태를 공유합니다. 'use client' 경계에서만 사용 가능하므로, 서버 컴포넌트에서는 컨텍스트를 직접 읽을 수 없습니다.

tour.title: 레이아웃 컴포넌트
tour.description: Navbar.tsx와 Footer.tsx는 layout.tsx에 마운트되어 모든 페이지에 나타나는 공통 UI 쉘입니다. Navbar는 모바일 메뉴와 i18n 토글을 포함하고, Footer는 KAIST 관련 외부 링크와 언어 전환 버튼을 제공합니다. 두 컴포넌트 모두 language-context에서 현재 언어를 읽어 텍스트를 렌더링합니다.

tour.title: 데이터베이스 스키마
tour.description: prisma/schema.prisma는 전체 데이터 모델의 단일 진실 원천(single source of truth)입니다. Notice, Resource, Building, BuildingFloor, Professor 등 13개 모델이 정의되어 있으며, 이후 등장하는 모든 API와 서버 컴포넌트가 이 스키마를 기반으로 데이터를 읽고 씁니다. 스키마를 먼저 파악하면 각 API가 어떤 데이터를 다루는지 즉시 이해할 수 있습니다.
tour.languageLesson: Prisma 스키마의 model 블록은 TypeScript 타입으로 자동 생성됩니다. @relation 지시어는 외래 키 관계를 선언하며, Prisma Client는 이를 바탕으로 include/select로 관계 데이터를 조인합니다.

tour.title: Prisma 클라이언트 설정
tour.description: lib/prisma.ts는 싱글턴 패턴으로 Prisma Client 인스턴스를 생성합니다. Turso(클라우드 libSQL)에 연결하기 위해 @prisma/adapter-libsql을 사용하며, prisma.config.ts에서 데이터소스 URL을 읽습니다. fan-in이 6으로 전체 코드베이스에서 가장 많이 임포트되는 파일로, 모든 데이터 접근의 허브 역할을 합니다.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js의 Hot Module Replacement 환경에서 매 모듈 reload마다 새 DB 연결이 생기는 것을 방지하기 위해 globalThis에 인스턴스를 캐싱하는 싱글턴 패턴을 사용합니다. Prisma v7은 schema.prisma에 datasource url 필드를 직접 쓰지 않고 prisma.config.ts에서 관리합니다.

tour.title: 인증 시스템
tour.description: lib/auth.ts는 NextAuth.js v5의 Credentials Provider를 구성합니다. 관리자 아이디/비밀번호를 환경 변수에서 검증하고, 성공 시 JWT 세션을 발급합니다. fan-in이 3으로 관리자 레이아웃, 관리자 대시보드, 파일 업로드 API가 이 모듈에 의존합니다.
tour.languageLesson: NextAuth v5는 Auth.js로도 불리며, 이전 버전과 달리 Edge Runtime을 지원합니다. JWT 전략을 쓰면 DB 없이 세션 검증이 가능하며, getServerSession 대신 auth() 헬퍼로 서버 컴포넌트에서 세션을 읽습니다.

tour.title: 홈 페이지
tour.description: app/page.tsx는 학생들이 처음 마주치는 메인 페이지입니다. lib/prisma.ts를 통해 최신 공지사항을 서버에서 쿼리하고 정적으로 렌더링하며, 자주 사용하는 링크들을 빠른 접근 버튼으로 배치합니다. 서버 컴포넌트로 동작하기 때문에 추가적인 API 요청 없이 초기 HTML에 데이터가 포함됩니다.

tour.title: 공지사항 API & 페이지
tour.description: app/api/notices/route.ts는 REST API 엔드포인트로, 공개 GET 요청은 누구나 접근할 수 있지만 POST(공지 등록)는 관리자 인증이 필요합니다. lib/validation.ts를 통해 입력값을 검증한 뒤 Prisma로 DB에 저장합니다. app/notices/page.tsx는 이 API와 같은 Prisma 쿼리를 서버에서 직접 실행해 공지 목록을 렌더링하는 서버 페이지입니다.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js App Router에서 route.ts는 HTTP 메서드 이름(GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE)을 export하는 Route Handler입니다. 같은 디렉터리 안에 page.tsx와 route.ts가 공존할 수 없으므로 API 핸들러는 app/api/ 하위에 분리됩니다.

tour.title: 서버 입력 검증
tour.description: lib/validation.ts는 API 핸들러에서 호출되는 서버 전용 유틸리티 모듈입니다. URL 형식, 문자열 길이, 금액 범위 등을 검증하는 함수를 제공하여 잘못된 데이터가 DB에 저장되는 것을 방지합니다. fan-in이 3으로 공지 API, 파일 업로드 API, FloorMapEditor가 이 모듈을 공통으로 사용합니다.

tour.title: 관리자 패널
tour.description: app/admin/(protected)/layout.tsx는 auth.ts로 세션을 확인하고, 인증되지 않은 요청은 로그인 페이지로 리다이렉트하는 가드 역할을 합니다. app/admin/(protected)/page.tsx는 인증 후 보이는 대시보드로 공지, 자료, 건물 정보 등 콘텐츠 관리 기능의 진입점입니다. Next.js의 route groups 기능을 활용해 URL 경로 없이 레이아웃 중첩을 구현합니다.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js의 (folderName) 문법은 route group으로, URL 경로에는 나타나지 않으면서 레이아웃을 공유하는 페이지들을 논리적으로 묶습니다. /admin/(protected)/page.tsx는 실제 URL상 /admin으로 접근됩니다.

tour.title: 파일 업로드 API
tour.description: app/api/upload/route.ts는 관리자가 이미지를 업로드할 때 사용하는 엔드포인트입니다. MIME 타입 검사와 sharp 라이브러리로 이미지 크기를 추출한 뒤, Vercel Blob 스토리지에 저장하고 URL을 반환합니다. auth.ts로 관리자 세션을 검증하고 validation.ts로 입력을 검사하여 무단 업로드를 차단합니다.

tour.title: 층별 지도 핵심 시스템
tour.description: 이 프로젝트의 가장 독창적인 기능은 건물 층별 인터랙티브 지도입니다. lib/svg-parser.ts는 업로드된 SVG를 XSS 공격 벡터(script, onload 등)로부터 sanitize하고 방 폴리곤을 감지합니다. lib/pathfinding.ts는 Dijkstra 알고리즘으로 두 방 사이의 최단 경로를 계산합니다. 이 두 유틸리티가 함께 FloorMapViewer의 안전한 렌더링과 경로 탐색을 뒷받침합니다.
tour.languageLesson: Dijkstra 알고리즘은 음수 가중치가 없는 그래프에서 단일 출발점 최단 경로를 O((V+E) log V)에 구합니다. SVG sanitization은 DOMParser로 파싱 후 허용 태그/속성 화이트리스트만 남기는 방식으로 XSS를 차단합니다.

tour.title: 지도 뷰어 & 에디터
tour.description: components/FloorMapViewer.tsx는 학생 대면 지도 컴포넌트로 SVG를 렌더링하고, 방을 클릭하면 교수 정보 팝업을 표시하며, svg-parser와 pathfinding을 사용해 경로를 시각적으로 그려줍니다. components/admin/FloorMapEditor.tsx는 관리자용 에디터로 SVG/이미지 업로드, 폴리곤 그리기, 내비게이션 그래프 편집 기능을 제공합니다. app/department-info/department-info-client.tsx는 건물 선택 UI와 교수 검색을 FloorMapViewer와 결합한 최종 사용자 페이지입니다.
